overview.title: Spec Auditing Skill
overview.description: Provide arcforge with a read-only, advisory-only audit skill for the SDD pipeline's upstream artifacts (design.md, spec.xml, details/, dag.yaml). The skill fills a gap documented in docs/research/sdd-v2-downstream-contract-gap.md: SDD v2's existing validation points are binary block-or-pass, leaving no channel for "notify the human, let them decide" feedback. arc-auditing-spec fans out three axis-aligned sub-agents, presents structured findings via AskUserQuestion U3 two-stage triage, and surrenders final decisions to the main session — never modifying upstream artifacts itself. v2 iteration tightens the ceremony layer: Phase 3 and Phase 4 fire only when they add value the Phase 2 report alone does not carry, extending the skill's existing graceful-degradation stance from the input layer (missing spec.xml or dag.yaml) to the output layer (too few HIGH findings to batch, too few resolutions to choose between).

includes.feature: Public contract of the /arc-auditing-spec skill: user-invoked invocation semantics, hard read-only boundaries enforced via tool grants, no pipeline auto-invocation, implementation delegated to arc-writing-skills.

Three parallel fan-out sub-agents (cross-artifact-alignment, internal-consistency, state-transition-integrity), their finding schema, axis-scope separation, and graceful degradation on missing inputs.

Markdown-table report format, U3 two-stage AskUserQuestion triage UX (multiSelect Stage 1, batched Stage 2 with diff previews), Decisions table output, and optional L4-path persistence via --save flag. v2 adds ceremony-threshold rules: Phase 3 only fires when N_HIGH >= 2, Phase 4 only fires per finding with >= 2 resolutions, Decisions table only renders when ceremony ran at all, and the Phase 2 Overview row carries visual emphasis when exactly one HIGH is present.

excludes.reason: Pipeline auto-invocation — brainstorming / refining / planning MUST remain unchanged; the skill is ad-hoc user-invoked (T1 decision).
Autonomous edits — reviewer agents never modify audited artifacts; post-audit execution belongs to the main session.
Code review / PR review — source-code review is handled by arc-requesting-review, arc-receiving-review, Claude Code /review, pr-review-toolkit; this skill only audits spec-family artifacts.
Cross-spec batch audit — one invocation audits one spec-id; multi-spec loops are user-driven, not built in.
Policy boundary around free-text edits to structured artifacts (Finding 2.5 in the gap report) — deferred to a separate future spec (arc-structured-artifact-policy).

Coordinator / engine bug fixes — A3 agent surfaces DAG state drift, but fixes to scripts/lib/coordinator.js belong to a separate engine-fix spec. The §6.5 downstream-contract gap (dag.yaml auto-commit vs. revert-after-merge) is explicitly out of scope for the v2 iteration.
Changes to upstream pipeline skills — arc-brainstorming / arc-refining / arc-planning and their Iron Laws are not touched by this spec.
